title: Keep long-running queue jobs invisible while their worker is alive
prompt:
Repair the worker heartbeat wiring in `lib/reliable_queue.rb` without changing the public queue or handler API. The in-memory queue leases each reserved job for a finite visibility timeout, and `Worker` passes its handler a callback to call between bounded pieces of long-running work. That callback is currently inert, so a healthy job can outlive its lease and execute twice. Each successful callback invocation must renew that worker's current lease for the worker's configured visibility timeout and return true. If renewal is rejected because the lease is no longer current, the callback must immediately raise `ReliableQueue::LostLease`.

Preserve the rest of the lease protocol: expiry must remain enabled so a worker that stops heartbeating becomes reservable at its deadline with a new attempt, owner, and token; only the matching worker and lease token may renew or complete a running attempt; stale completion must not overwrite the newer attempt; and completing the same successfully completed lease more than once must be idempotent and retain its first result. Do not serialize workers, use wall-clock sleeps, or weaken token checks. Keep `ManualClock`-based behavior deterministic, use only the Ruby standard library, and do not modify the protected tests. Success is `sh test/verify.sh` from the repository root; verification must remain offline and deterministic.
